reviews.review_text: Playing as a little orange cat reminded me so much of my own orange boy that passed away.It’s such a lovely game.

A charming little adventure about a ginger-tailed cat, filled with friendship at its very best and a quiet sense of sadness softened by hope. The visual style is both unique and incredibly atmospheric, giving the entire journey a warm yet melancholic feel. Short, memorable and genuinely heartfelt.

10 knocked-over vases out of 10.

My cats approve it 10/10

What a wonderful Indie game. The atmosphere, the robots, the soundtracks, the puzzles - incredible and memorable. 

As a cat owner I approve and recommend this game very much!

[b]Stray on Steam is the undisputed champion of the highly niche "feline cyberpunk dystopian" genre, finally letting you fulfill the dream of navigating a neon-soaked metropolis as a remarkably resilient orange tabby. Instead of burdening you with heavy arsenals and the fate of the universe, the game gives you much simpler priorities: finding your way to the surface, partnering up with a surprisingly helpful drone named B-12, and purposefully knocking paint cans onto the heads of unsuspecting humanoid robots. The gameplay loop is a wonderfully atmospheric blend of vertical platforming and light puzzle-solving, featuring animations so incredibly fluid and accurate that your real-life pets will likely stare at your monitor in deep confusion. While B-12 handles all the tedious hacking and translating, you are left entirely free to focus on crucial cat duties, like aggressively scratching up digital rugs, finding a cozy cardboard box for a nap, and occasionally outrunning swarms of terrifying mutant bacteria called Zurks.

As a PC title, it runs beautifully whether you are pushing the gorgeous lighting engine to its limits or curling up on the couch with a Steam Deck. The roughly six-to-eight-hour adventure is tightly paced, delivering an emotionally resonant narrative that never overstays its welcome or bogs you down with bloated side quests and unnecessary skill trees. There is something profoundly therapeutic about exploring a beautifully decaying, robot-inhabited slum and realizing that even after humanity is long gone, a cat's biological imperative to push a glass cup off a high ledge remains absolute. It is a visually stunning, deeply charming must-play that perfectly captures the unbridled chaos of being a feline, complete with a dedicated "meow" button that you are essentially obligated to spam during serious cinematic moments.[/b]
I previously played this game on the PS4, and when I saw it was on Steam I really wanted to play it again. I don't regret the choice to re-purchase this game.

Bloody hell! It's a surprisingly good and touching story, and not even just because of the cat. I went in completely blind, so I wouldn't say Stray exceeded my expectations since I didn't have many. But it certainly delivered more than I could have hoped for. Stray is definitely a game I'll remember.

It's not that long, which is why I waited four years for a substantial discount. At the same time, it doesn't overstay its welcome - which is great. They told a good story and didn't extend it needlessly for an extra 5 to 10 hours like many developers do.

👍😻🐈

This is a story driven game!
I enjoyed my little adventure with Stray. It managed to satisfy an interest story wise and a small thrill of adventure. It is a short game, story with basic exploration should take you around 8 hours mark with 100% can push you to 11/12 hours probably if you did not optimize from the start.

The Good:
- Story, I found myself asking questions and making theories about what is going on, was well made.
- World is beautiful and was well done, especially the cities, I found myself explorating the allies/roofs and trying to see everything.
- The concept of being a cat is strong, you can climb a lot of things and jump off places, it was decently done.

The Bad:
- Rushed story/encounters. In some situations when you meet and important figure or a scenario it feels so rushed, you can really feel it on the almost none existing chatter and the "skip".
- Most puzzles are very simple, maybe it's experience speaking but I don't remember a single one that was close to a challenge.
- This is an opinion but I would say the ending.
- The price is insane for an 8 hours gameplay, overpriced.


Fun and short game I would recommend this game but get it on an omega sale because it is overpriced like crazy.

Every district feels distinct, from neon-lit slums and crowded marketplaces to abandoned industrial complexes. The environmental storytelling is exceptional, revealing the fate of humanity through subtle details rather than lengthy exposition.

The world feels lived-in, and simply wandering through its streets is often as rewarding as completing objectives.

Movement is smooth and intuitive. Jumping between rooftops, squeezing through tight spaces, and knocking objects off shelves captures feline behavior remarkably well.

The game wisely avoids overcomplicated mechanics. Instead, it emphasizes immersion and discovery.

[b]Stray[/b] remains visually impressive thanks to its detailed environments and atmospheric lighting. Reflections from neon signs, rain-soaked streets, and cluttered interiors create a believable cyberpunk setting.

The soundtrack complements the mood perfectly, alternating between ambient electronic tracks and quieter emotional moments. Combined with excellent sound design, the game creates a strong sense of place.

[b]Stray[/b] succeeds because it focuses on what makes its concept special. Rather than relying on gimmicks, it delivers a thoughtful adventure with memorable characters, stunning environments, and genuine emotional weight.

It may not offer deep combat systems or complex gameplay, but its atmosphere and charm make it one of the most distinctive adventure games of recent years.

Whether you're a cat lover, a fan of cyberpunk worlds, or simply someone who enjoys well-crafted storytelling, this is a journey worth taking.

[h1]9/10[/h1]
DISASTER | BAD | MEDIOCRE | OKAY | GOOD | GREAT | [b][u]AMAZING [/u][/b]| MASTERPIECE
[quote]
Reviewed on: Win11 Home 64-bit, Intel i5-11600K, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5060 Ti 16GB PRIME, 32GB DDR4-3600 RAM, 2 x Kingston NV1 1TB M.2 NVMe SSD, Internet Broadband 1000/1000 Mbit[/quote]
[quote]
If you like this review, then please consider giving it a thumbs up. I've also reviewed other games that you might find interesting. If so please follow [u][url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/27418263/] Top of the Chart.[/url][/u]

Me-Wow! Such a beautiful game in such a dystopian world for this little adventurer! Absolutely loved this game! <3

This would be a good movie if it ever gets made!

I was promised a game about little kitty in a big city but got postapocalyptic, dystopian world filled with death, cruelty and emotional damage and it was worth It. Experience is really deep and the worldbuilding tells a really good story. Although I'd recommend you to play Stray with achievements in your mind - you will get much more knowledge about world around you while doing so.

What a unique game!
Absolutely a wonderful journey! Even though the gameplay was quite simple, it was still very fun. The puzzles are decent—simple and easy, yet enjoyable—and the game visually is absolutely beautiful. 

On the other hand, while the story wasn't a complete masterpiece, it's a solid 7/10 for me. Overall, I’d give the entire experience a well-deserved 8/10.

I 100%-ed the game, and I don't regret it one bit! It's actually the only game I've played where the collectibles genuinely have a story behind them, rather than just collecting random stuff.

Stray is not any game. If you will let yourself travel, talk with NPC's and absorb world-building around you, you will have unforgettable experience. Every single place is mesmerasing in it's own way and could be your new wallpaper.
Attention to detail, soundtrack, hidden lore, even shift of perspective - you play as a cat, you don't speak, but Stray has a lot to say. Connection between you and others, learning memories of your companion, helping strangers. 
For me Stray is emobodiement of postapocalyptic cyberpunk. Atmosphere is just unmatched. I can see that this game was done with love towards it and every detail of it. It in itself makes Stray worth playing.

You can meow, you can scratch, you can destroy, you can do everything cat is supposed to do. A lot of parkour, some sneaking around, some running away. Riddles were really easy, just be attentive. Nothing too hard, but I think it works like a favor for Stray - you can really immerse yourself into this game. Which you should.

There were some bugs - mostly bugged jumping, once or twice NPC didn't move and I had to reload checkpoint.
Achievements were really well done - they help you absorb this world even more and guide you smoothly through the game.

I'll start with the pros:
- really impressed with what the indie devs did visually here. On par with top tier studios

- I am the cat lady that this appeals to. I *love* getting to run around as a cat, knock things over, stick my head in a paper bag, scratch at doors to be let out, nuzzle against robots to get them to display heart faces and make happy robot chirps, and run through pool balls to make them roll around. The devs clearly know and love cats and it shows.

- As someone with hypermobile joints and dyspraxia that makes reaction timing hard, I really appreciate the auto-jumping. I know a lot of folks hate this, and it's a pro for me.

- I've read other reviews describe this game as hand-holdy and I think that's a fair complaint. For me, I like how obvious many of the aspects of the interactions and puzzle solving are because I'm not interested in this game to have a big think or a high stress combat experience. I just want to run around as a cat and have fun doing it.

Which brings me to my cons:
- Again, with the context that button mashing is murder on hypermobile joints and dyspraxia makes precision timing challenging, the combat runs against the zurks are game ruining for me. I am stuck in chapter 7 because after about 20 tries (and therefore having a clear understanding of the pathing) I can't get past them. I didn't time how much of my 3.9 hours of playtime was spent on the zurk sections and I wish I had that data. I just kept saying to myself 'this isn't fun' and finally quit in frustration.

- As others have mentioned, the gameplay might not be worth the price for those on tight budgets (with an acknowledgment that devs deserve to be paid and indie companies probably have a different funding structure than AAA companies? I'm not sure how all of that works). For me, I would have already returned this game if I had paid full price for it.

- Some have also mentioned that the puzzles are too simple, the exploration too railroaded, and this basically being a fetch quest game. These wouldn't be cons to me, personally (because I like a cozy little game with a post-apocalyptic tone), if the combat didn't feel impossible. I know I'm not great at real-time combat (I'm among the only 37% of players who have died 9+ times in this game), and I've spent over 1,000 hours in RDR2, about 200 in Death Stranding, and about 60 in Outer Worlds and haven't died as much in a single run in any of those games as I have in the Dead End run in Stray. 

Maybe this game just isn't for me, but I don't think that's true. If the zurks were about 25-50% less hard, I'd say this game was precisely made for me (though I'd prefer they were absent, because in my opinion they're mucking up a perfectly cozy cat game). 

There's so much to love about this game and I'm so frustrated with it that I'm considering getting it refunded, unfinished, because I cannot get past a single combat run, which makes me worried for future combat encounters. 

tl;dr - great cat content, not great accessibility for those with mild+ physical disability

You could say Stray is in a class of its own as one of my favorite games. I was hooked from the get go, taken with the characters and the world and the atmosphere all at once. It puts you right in the middle of its cyberpunk setting; for all intents and purposes you are out there in the city as a stray cat.

There is a certain simplicity to the way it tells its story but it is no less emotional for that. Each part of the map has its own sense of mystery and personality. I found myself going to every nook and cranny of the game to put in the work for the memories and achievements.

It is an experience well worth your while if you want something as original as this, be it for the narrative or the collectibles. The kind of game that lingers in your mind after the credits have finished. If the devs ever put together a sequel I would be on it in a second.

A very simple story, and by no means long, but every minute of it is packed with tons of atmosphere, immersion, exploration, and puzzles just challenging enough to feel satisfied when you complete them, but not so difficult that you should be stumped.
Sometimes the biggest challenge is just navigating the areas and not getting lost (this is only slightly problematic in 2 "city" areas, while the remainder are mostly linear)
There are a few frustrating elements if you're going after all the achievements, but otherwise the game is cozy and relaxing, even the pursuit sequences can be handled with limited stress. Perhaps the best way to play is to simply be a cat. Explore at your leisure, bother people, run from what chases you (and when you do run, weave and zigzag)
The controls all lend to this aspect so well that I find myself nit-picking over trivialities, such as wanting to have the limbs positioned better when standing on stairs (facing up or down), wanting a dedicated "sit" button, and/or wanting more ability to control the cat's head focus.
It's well worth putting a full day aside to soak up every bit the game has to offer, and don't worry; the game does not play for you.

Fun little game. Lots of jumping around. Some levels can get really frustrating especially the ones in the sewers and the jails. Could benefit from manual saving. Beautiful to look at and has a good story!
Good game, its short but it has a great story and some cool areas to explore. Looks great on a oled.

I honestly really enjoyed the story in Stray. The graphics were amazing, and the quality of the game was incredible. I bought the game while I was on a road trip because I brought my computer with me and wanted something fun to play during the drive. I came across Stray, and it ended up being one of the best games I’ve ever played.

I loved everything about it
Oi mate it's a ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ BLAST, cried me ass off when the red cat fell. Oi oi can't wait to finish me game!
Can meow , scratch and knock all the cups into the ground for literally no reason. Good game and I'm a cat.

This game was honestly way more emotional than I expected.

The world and soundtrack are beautiful, and exploring everything as a cat somehow feels really calming. I also loved just running around knocking stuff over and scratching every carpet possible like a real cat.

By the end I was actually a little emotional and attached. Definitely worth playing if you like atmospheric games with a unique vibe.
epic car game, cyberpunk aesthetic type shiii which is my fave aesthetic type shii okay thanks

What a lovely game start to finish
World design is extremely detailed and full of life
Story is compelling
Some dark and horror themed aspects to keep it spicy
But best of all, cat!

Stray was honestly one of the most unique and emotional games I’ve played in a long time.

What started as “a cool game where you play as a cat” turned into a beautiful, atmospheric story with incredible world-building, emotional moments, amazing music, and surprisingly deep lore. The environments were stunning, the movement felt smooth and natural, and the relationship between the cat and B-12 made the ending hit way harder than I expected.

Even with one funny physics bug along the way 😂, this game was absolutely worth playing from start to finish.

The developers created a world that somehow feels lonely, cozy, creepy, peaceful, and emotional all at once. Exploring the city, helping robots, escaping Zurks, and uncovering the story kept me hooked the entire time.

If you love atmospheric adventure games, emotional storytelling, cyberpunk vibes, or cats… play this game.

10/10 🐈💙
Absolute peak game cried at the end this is the best game ive ever played the rating should be even higher than 97% and you dont need high end specs i have 90 fps rx580 8gb max settings 10/10 game

Solid game, I'll be honest I still don't quite know what I played. However, it was beautiful and managed to make me feel emotions. I'd recommend it.

It also runs well on Linux.

you get to rp as a cat and nuzzle against cute robots, what else could you wish for?

the incredible cyberpunk dystopian atmosphere that evokes an odd warmth within me that i have yet to find in another game, i guess :)
A surprisingly deep & rich story with pretty good gameplay to go along with it as well. Also that ending man, I almost cried (And that's saying something).

Genuinely worth every penny! Played this when it released, I was anxiously waiting for it to be playable and it did not disappoint once. Stunning visuals, moving story, great gameplay/mechanics and silly ROBOTS! 

If you want something more exploratory and slightly puzzle based this is a really great game, especially if you have an affinity for post-apocalyptic settings and cats!
Alguna vez llegue a pensar que una historia de gatos y robots trabajando juntos me iba a conmover tanto? No. Lo hizo? Absolutamente.

Stray has a lot to say and does so without any lecturing. It may sometimes tell you, but it always also shows you and oh, the images are powerful: moral, societal, environmental themes abound and the messages are delivered through the lens of a masterfully crafted story. Stray's world feels truly unique. At times the gameplay is of a more exploratory, slower-paced kind, at others, it is fast and loaded with action. Intertwined with it all - an everpresent feeling of mystery and foreboding. The game had my attention from the very start until the very end. 

As mentioned, the gameplay alternates between two modes - one of curious exploration and one of intense action keeping you on your paws - both to the effect that you just cannot get bored - it never gets to the point of feeling like being locked in an endless puzzle-solving maze, nor does it tire you out by forcing you to constantly fight for your nine lives. It just works. You know when you get to chill and when its about to get dangerous. Although the game is said to work better with a console, I played it on a PC with a keyboard and it did not feel weird at all.

It also looks spectacular, and feels real. I love the environment: it's darkened and grimy with age, so familliar and yet, at the same time, futuristically alien. I love the atmosphere and its complexity: despite the buzz of activity all around you, the game manages to deliver undertones of deep loneliness. Something is missing all along and you can feel it. The soundtrack complements it all nicely, and the theme alone is an eerie masterpiece. To top it all, yes, you can play as a little furball, which is a special kind of joy on its own, but it is also a wonderful choice from the narrative perspective. It is part of the message.

All in all, as you can already tell, I think that the world crafted by Stray and the story it tells are definitely worth experiencing at least once. That said, it was over rather soon - I took my time with it all and still finished it in 10 hours. But if you get it on sale, like I did, I do not think you'll be disappointed.

Catssasin's Creed Cyberpunk 
Beautiful game worth playing ! 🐾

Holy story. For 30 probably not worth. But this story hits really hard, and is great for 15 dollars (I got it half off). There really isn't too much content, but the environment, and the layers of story sprinkled in are great. Hit me on an emotional level that it shouldnt have, but it did
Im a little late to the party but arguably the best game ive played this year. The atmosphere and all the levels were beautiful.
what amazing game, i liked how the cat moves, run, and the world was awesome and designed pretty well and story was good too. so i like it even my cat like it and she's worth every single dollar.

One of the greatest games I've ever played. Amazing story, amazing art direction, amazing world building, and cat.

Only complaint is it got really blurry during the ending.
Pleasing visuals but boring gameplay. You get to be a cat and do kitty cat shenanigans sometimes. Everything else about this game is just okay.
Fantastic story telling and wonderful visuals.

This is a quirkmaxxing cutepilled platforming/puzzle/horror adventure game, where you play as a cat, created by two buddies who escaped Ubisoft Montpellier to try and make something worthwhile of their lives.

 The backstory is a somewhat common one: humans were exterminated by a pandemic, and earth is now inhabited by AIs, who are trying to build a civilisation of their own by emulating the extinct humans society. A bunch of games did this and IMHO none quite as well as The Talos Principle.
However, Stray doesn't take itself very seriously and adopts a Saturday morning cartoon type of narration, which works really well here. Too many games try to be deep and fail, ending up being really dumb instead. Not this one.
Stray has tragic undertones but keeps it light and playful most of the time, and it serves the game well. The gameplay isn't very original — you talk to robots, they give you quests, sometimes you gotta jump and climb, sometimes a bunch of monsters start chasing you and you have to evade them... but you're a cat. Actually a cat AND a drone named B12 which acts a bit like Navi in Ocarina Of Time (old people will get this reference). The drone helps you communicate with AIs, unlock doors, etc.

This game isn't the most memorable but it also doesn't have any major flaws. Graphics are good, controls too, music is nice, quests aren't boring... It's nothing crazy but it's solid fun from start to finish. Recommended without hesitation.
The game was fun and sweet-ish, but the ending is the reason for me not recommending this game. I can say no more, but hopefully it won't affect you the way it affected me.
This game is really fun to play and it runs great on my machine. The story line is incredible and you get to explore. it does not force you to follow the story like other games do
amazing! loved the mechanics and graphics... please add a free play option so we can run around and explore the levels with the roof open and explore more and go back to find missing memories.

Genuinely an amazing game. You wouldn't think there's a story, or that there wouldn't be anything meaningful in this game, but there was. Had me tearing up at the end. I hope to get all the achievements for this game, it's worth every minute playing.

My FAVORITE game of all time. It feels so nostalgic from the first second you launch the game. The developers did an amazing job and made a beautiful and detailed game! Be prepared to laugh and cry as you help the friends you make along the way!

I've just played for a couple hours and I adore this game! It's quite immersive, and I find myself able to do many cat things that I didn't expect, but I'll let you discover that on your own. Mostly puzzle solving, but there are some action parts too, don't get too discouraged if those are hard, just keep trying and you'll get the hang of it. I'm running it on a really old system with a pretty crappy GFX card and it's been mostly very smooth, so no worries there.

What an absolute joy to play!

Cute and engaging, Stray is a fun little adventure in a dystopian world. Everything is grimy and dead, but there's a glimmer of hope with this little orange furball.

Satisfying movement, beautiful setting, lovely soundtrack and great worldbuilding. Somehow felt nostalgic; reminded me of the old Oddworld games, but without the violence.
ending should be extended overall theme of the game is very good alll characters are well designed

If you are a mouse and keyboard player beware.  There is a segment that must be punishment for using a mouse.  Apparently controllers continue to work just fine, only mouse is disabled.  This is a chase segment that needs movement control and on off left right with a fixed camera make this unenjoyable to the point that the game is not worth it.  Apparently this is widely posted problem that is not fixed so it must be intentional.
Very nice and immersive storytelling. Stunning graphics. Interesting perspective, running around as a feline. Funny easter eggs. Not too demanding puzzle-wise nor platformer-wise as to frustrate.

orange cat activities
also my pc is sorta crap and this runs really good (graphics max btw)
meow

I don't even usually leave reviews but this game was so BEAUTIFUL. 10/10 genuinely left in awe of this magnificent world building, environment design and wholesome story. It was very relaxing to play with some tense moments but the gameplay wasn't too difficult so I never found myself getting stuck at a certain point, which I liked.
Honestly this game is so fun and cute, I lost my spark for games a few years ago but stray really brought me back into it, I have my old flow again.

This game shows us that no living thing is too small, and no goal is too big.
We start by controlling a cat, an experience that lets us see things from its perspective: how it sees, how it moves, and how it perceives its surroundings.
At first, we find ourselves in a world full of color, alongside a group with whom we share a bond. However, we quickly face the tragedy of separation and the uncertainty of being left alone to face the unknown.
We enter a gray and dark world, with no one to accompany us.
Little by little, we begin to understand where we are, and even though mishaps arise along the way, we keep moving forward. Then we encounter those who, in a way, are our equals: the robots.
They don’t have four legs and they communicate in zeros and ones, but they share something essential: the feeling of loneliness.
Some robots from The Suburbs dream of reaching the Center; those who live in the Center don’t even know they’re completely enclosed. And we, like a cat, still don’t know where our family is.
We find ourselves in a gray world that, slowly and almost without realizing it, we are illuminating. We leave a trail of light in those who help us: through a photograph, through music, through reunions, and through hope.
This work reminds us that, as long as we have someone to accompany us, we will never truly be a stray.

Este juego viene a decirnos que no hay ser vivo tan pequeño ni objetivo tan grande.
Comenzamos controlando a un gato, experiencia que nos permite asomarnos a su punto de vista: cómo ve, cómo se mueve y cómo percibe lo que lo rodea.
Al principio estamos en un mundo lleno de color, junto a un grupo con el que compartimos un vínculo. Sin embargo, rápidamente nos enfrentamos a la tragedia de la separación y a la incertidumbre de quedar solos frente a lo desconocido.
Entramos en un mundo gris y oscuro, sin nadie que nos acompañe.
Poco a poco empezamos a comprender dónde estamos y, aunque surjan percances en el camino, seguimos avanzando. Entonces encontramos a quienes, de alguna manera, son nuestros iguales: los robots.
No tienen cuatro patas y se comunican en ceros y unos, pero comparten algo esencial: el sentimiento de soledad.
Algunos robots de Los Suburbios sueñan con llegar al Centro; los que viven en el Centro ni siquiera saben que están completamente encerrados. Y nosotros, como gato, seguimos sin saber dónde está nuestra familia.
Nos encontramos en un mundo gris que, lentamente y casi sin darnos cuenta, vamos iluminando. Dejamos un rastro de luz en quienes nos ayudan: a través de una fotografía, de la música, de reencuentros y de la esperanza.
Esta obra nos recuerda que, mientras tengamos a alguien que nos acompañe, nunca estaremos realmente perdidos.


Have a good day.
Stray is genuinely one of the best games ive played. It has alot of things i like! Post apocolyptic themes, cats, parasites ect! I wish we would get a sequel if the cat reunited with its family ^^
What an amazing game. I see how this nearly won GOTY in 2022. Its cat-tastic! Its cozy and engaging

A really nice simplistic game, with a quite interesting experience to offer. It's not something new to say that experiencing the story through a Cat perspective is a really interesting concept, I also like how this game had a lot of diverse feeling in it, with some section had a fun yet simple action, a relaxing vibe with interesting set of challenge, and a good thematic that was explored without beating you over the head to across it's point. Also besides the particular cat, this game also full of interesting characters of NPC that to my opinion give such a color to a game with an already beautifully colorfull art direction.

A very good game, I'd honestly call it almost a masterpiece. Comparing to RDR2, the mechanics itself aren't AS good which is obviously expected, but the story is absolutely fantastic. Definetly worth it's price.
Finally finished stray. And all I can say is if your thinking about buying it. BUY IT. it is in my top 10 games oat and is the only game to ever make me cry.
Very relaxing game with an alright story, but whats most suprising is the visuals, overall very worth on sale!

I loved every second of it.

Every glaze this game gets is not close to what it deserves.

+cat

I really didn't have a high expectation about this game and simply chose to play because I like cats. It turns out to be one of my GOAT game ever. I regretting not recording the game play for myself and watch it later.

i streamed the game on twitch and have clips from it all.   It's truthfully a great story.  Great gameplay.  Some parts got me stressed but it's definitely a amazing game.  i highly recommend.   I completed the game in 6.4 hours.   However, I will go back at some point to get all the achievements.   Only thing I advise on is prepare yourself for a rollercoaster ride of emotions at the end.
It's a great game, very fun and exciting-- love the art and premise.
This game has an amazing storyline, not to hard of puzzles and the characters are amazing! I Would totally recommend this game to anyone! :)

10/10
Don't let the cute cat fool you and give up in the beginning, at first, it feels like a random game just for kids, but the further you dive into it, the more you see how deep, beautiful, and detailed it actually is. It completely met my expectations. I highly recommend it to absolutely everybody. It's an ideal game.

I actually loved playing this game! I was pleased with the difficulty of the game honestly. I really went in expecting it to be pretty easy. I recommend it.  It was super cute as well. To bad its not multiplayer, I feel like all games should have that option now days.
"A beautiful game with even more enjoyable gameplay and stunning graphics!

What's not to love here? - easy to grasp controls, a really interesting world, super cute NPCs with fun interactions, some tricky sections for those looking for a bit of adrenaline, and a brave little cat who just wants to help.

I may have cried, but it was totally worth it. If you're going back and forth on getting this game, do yourself a favour and just go for it!

It's such a cute game with a good storyline and small side quests to do. I loved interacting with every single robot. It may not be a long game, but I still got so attached to each of the characters which goes to show they are great characters.

its good, but i sill dont know what to do most the time in the game.

Genuinely delightful game. Good twist, lots of unexpected stuff. very short game but very worth it when on sale. 100 percented for the love of the game.
a cozy, cool indie game but it can be short lived for 30 dollars (i got it for 15 hahaaaahhhhh)but its totally good

One of the best games i've played so far, great for people the are introverted or just want to play a game alone. Although the game play could have been a little bit longer in my opinion for a paying game but the graphics and everything are amazing.
Whole game is fun, cute, and the environments are super well done. Great if you love a game with good atmosphere and a world that actually feels lived in
short game, but it was enjoyable wish i had one of backpacks for my own cat so we could communicate.
Amazing game. Wish it was longer but still beyond satisfied with it.

This is what a definition of a word MASTERPIECE is.

A game with an absorbing and beautiful story that genuinely made me cry at the end, filled with a lot of hope, dread and other feelings and emotions that will get you feeling sad but hopeful in the end. Honestly one of the better stories out there, with interesting plots and plot-twists I didn't expect coming at all.

An innovative and never seen gameplay before that made this whole experience so great. You play as a cat and do cat things like throwing stuff from shelves, edges on to the ground or into the void. Also scratching rugs, jumping high and meow a lot... and much much more!. There were a few well-made chase sequences, as well as great sneaky parts that add some variety to the gameplay to make it less samey which I thought were great and had a lot of fun with them. Just that you can play as a cat alone already bumps up the score for me to 9/10.

Amazing world-building that gets you engaged in the world around you, finding clues (memories) and piecing the whole picture of what went wrong in this cyberpunky filled city with robots, zurks and sentinels and why things are, as they are. Also the attention to detail made this whole world look gorgeous, nothing feels empty or defective. The lighting is done very well and is very pleasing to look at. There is a lot to explore on your own and just overall the places you will explore feel very completed and nothing feels out of the place.

I got really attached to a few characters, like the whole Outsiders team and our little flying robot companion helping us out and guiding us through this whole demolished by garbage and zurks city called "The Slums" and later on in Midtown. There were no moments I found someone annoying or ruining the experience. Every robot had it's own personality, even if some of them didn't say much.

Through my entire playthrough I never encountered any bugs, lags, or crashes. Making this a smooth 60 fps and no-bug experience.

Overall this is a 10/10 game, straight up. I don't have ANY insults or things I didn't like. It's a perfect game. Everything is done well to a little minor details. You can clearly see the passion that went into this game and that developers made sure this is gonna be an amazing experience for everyone. Hoping there will be a sequel one day, because ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ deserves it. Peace out.

A sci-fi game where you get to play as a cat? I already knew I was going to love it from the very beginning but still, wow. Everything is just beautifully done - the vibe, the story, the sound effects, the tension... I love how detailed and diverse the environment is, I was sooo happy every time I could explore a new area :D and the music always comes just at the right time to give you even more chills. Even the homepage is amazing, like sometimes I launch the game just to see it (extreme? naah)
Also kitty is just too cute for this world.
So yeah. 100% recommend.
insanely good.  Innovative but enjoyable.  great story but peripheral to just great gameplay

There's a lot of amazing things about this game: The music, the atmosphere that the game provides, the worldbuilding and the actual way the game is played. I thoroughly enjoyed playing the game and did my best to do all the little achievements and side quests available. I will say this though, I would only get this game when it is on sale. There isn't enough content for me to want to play it again in the near future. If you're just looking for a quick 5-hour adventure of a feline in a dystopian world, then this game is for you! But again, I would wait for a sale because I wouldn't have paid $30 for this game.

Control scheme is too bad.
All nice that they write it's ideally used with a gamepad but honestly people have made working keyboard controls for jump'n'runs for over 30 years. it's not ok to leave it like it is.

Such a lovely atmospheric game with gorgerous dystopic story. Enjoyed every minute.
Highly tempting to behave exactly like a cat and knock over everything what is not glued to surface.

It's MEOW out of 10.

One of the best game i played, I only played for few minutes and i am ready to recommend it to others, i haven't watched the game story but i dont think in the end the CAR will meet its family :( which make me feel sad

I remember watching DanTDM's playthrough when the game came out and wishing i could play this game myself. Well it certainly lives up to it 9.8/10 love it the graphics imo couldve done with a filter or two to make it look like a documentary but thats personal opinion i otherwise love the look gameplay and so far the story, as i havent finished the game yet. :)

Помню, столько бубнежа было по поводу игры в своё время. Забавно что теперь тоже самое с Mixtape от этого же издателя. Хочу сказать, игра то хорошая. Да простая но привлекательная.
Загадки довольно интересные и в основном связанные с окружением, что синергирует с игрой за кота.
В целом вайб постапока приятный, есть над чем поразмыслить.
Конечно, кот встроенный в наратив это костыль, ведь ведёт он себя как кот и через нас как игрок, но какая задумка разрабов. Не сказать что это плохо, но да, можно было бы что-то придумать.
В любом случае, игрушка приятная.

Fun game to play and figure out!
Managed to get through over 3 days- about 10 hours logged which is surprising to me because I rarely make it through games completely. Disclaimer to that, I didn't unlock all of the memories with B-12

Such a fun little  game! The graphics are lovely,  the environment is interesting and creepy, the  story is compelling, and I even sniffled a little at the end.  This game is  not too  long  either- there  is a trophy  for finishing it in under 2 hours.  I took  much longer than  that  (of  course), but that's because I  didn't know  where everything was. One  of  the main reasons I enjoy  replaying  is because it  is short and sweet. It can be completed in an  afternoon- I completed  2 runs within  the  same day.  So none of that 35+ hour nonsense.

The game  does a great  job of making you feel like a small  cat in a  big world, but without making  an open world-type clunker where you feel like everything is too big and traveling takes forever. 

The controls are easy and it's not a super violent or gory kind of game, but the sequences with enemies  managed to be thrilling in their own way none the less. I got just as agitated playing this as I might in Assassin's  Creed.   Poor kitty....

So check this out and experience the epic magnificence  that is cat.

Great game, if your looking for a short story with easy and very simple puzzles this is the game only took about 3.5 hours to complete, the only downside is in the ending  you really don't get to see how every one ends up and its leaves you on a cliff hanger but not a bad cliff hanger.
Love this game! Great storyline. Challenging puzzles. I did not want it to end. Highly recommended.
I want more games similar to this one! (and portal) besides the cat being just cute i just love the vibes and gameplay!

10/10 game MUST PLAY.
Brilliant aesthetics, heart-breaking story, banger soundtrack

I very much enjoyed the graphics and the story line. It was very easy for me to follow as a person who is just starting to get into gaming. I'll definitely be replaying as I'm sure there were things I missed in the first playthrough.
the ending is emotional but the story always takes a turn and makes sure its not easy
